Billing

Headway vs alma therapists: which insurance platform actually pays better?

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 6 min read

# Headway vs alma therapists: which insurance platform actually pays better? You went into private practice to focus on therapy, not to spend evenings on hold with insurance companies. If credentialing, chasing claims, and verifying benefits are eating your clinical hours, you’ve probably googled “headway vs alma therapists” more than once. Both companies promise to handle the insurance maze for you, but one is free and the other costs $125 a month. One gets you paid in 7 days, the other in 14. One has a directory that can fill your caseload overnight, the other gives you practice tools like telehealth and EHR-lite. Below I break down exactly what you get—and what you give up—with each platform so you can pick the one that fits your practice style and income goals. > **Quick verdict:** Headway is free and drives more referrals, but pays 5–15% less and requires 10+ weekly insurance clients. Alma charges $125/mo, negotiates higher rates, and throws in HIPAA telehealth and EHR features—worth it if you want higher reimbursement and practice tools without volume limits. --- ## Headway pricing Headway is 100% free to therapists. There are no platform fees, no per-session charges, and no credit-card hold for “incidentals.” You only pay the usual insurance write-offs. ## Headway features - Credentialing and re-credentialing handled for you (2–4 weeks) - Claims submission, ERA posting, and payment reconciliation run automatically - Real-time eligibility checks (no phone calls) - Basic HIPAA-compliant telehealth built in - Directory listing that Headway markets aggressively—over 60% of therapists report that most of their insurance clients find them this way - Payer mix: BCBS, Aetna, Cigna, United, Oscar, Humana, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid (8 national plans) - Available in 31 states ## Headway pros - Zero overhead - Fast, automated credentialing - High directory referral volume - Clean, modern dashboard - No need to chase claims or ERAs ## Headway cons - Reimburses 50–80% of billed charges—often 5–15% lower than Alma - Limited to 8 national payers; no Kaiser, TRICARE, or regional plans - Must maintain 10+ weekly insurance sessions or risk deactivation - No EHR, scheduling, or practice management extras - Support via chat/email only—no phone line ## Headway is best for Solo therapists who want a free, turnkey way to get in-network fast and who can reliably keep 10+ insurance slots full every week. If you don’t need EHR or group-practice bells and whistles and prefer marketing handled for you, Headway is the lowest-barrier entry to insurance panels. --- ## Alma pricing Alma costs $125 per month after a 30-day free trial. There is no per-session fee and no revenue share. ## Alma features - Full credentialing and re-credentialing support (4–8 weeks) - Claims filing and follow-up included—but you verify benefits manually - HIPAA-compliant telehealth with group-practice rooms - Lightweight EHR (notes, templates, secure messaging) - Client-matching algorithm and directory that supplies ~25% of average caseload - Payer mix: 15+ plans including Kaiser, TRICARE West, and large regional carriers - Available in all 50 states - Group-practice permission levels and collaboration notes - Optional EHR integrations at extra cost (e.g., SimplePractice) ## Alma pros - Negotiates rates 5–15% higher than Headway on average - Broader payer mix—good if you need Kaiser or TRICARE - No minimum session requirement—keep one client or 100 - Built-in telehealth and EHR-lite save software costs elsewhere - Dedicated account manager for live support ## Alma cons - $125 monthly fee after trial - Manual benefits verification adds admin time - Directory drives lower referral volume than Headway - Credentialing slower (up to 8 weeks) - EHR integrations cost extra ## Alma is best for Therapists who want higher reimbursement, a wider payer mix, and integrated practice tools without session minimums. If you’re willing to pay $125/mo to avoid juggling multiple software subscriptions and you don’t mind doing your own benefits checks, Alma gives you more control and higher revenue per session. --- ## Head-to-head comparison | Feature | Headway | Alma | |---------|---------|------| | Platform cost | Free | $125/mo after 30-day trial | | Average reimbursement (90834 BCBS TX) | $98 | $108 | | Payer count | 8 major | 15+ including regional | | States available | 31 | 50 | | Time from session to payout | 14 days | 7 days | | Directory referral share | >60% | ~25% | | Minimum insurance sessions | 10/week | None | | Practice tools | Basic telehealth | Telehealth + EHR-lite + group tools | | Support | Chat/email | Dedicated account manager | --- ## Who should choose Headway? Pick Headway if you: - Need zero overhead while you build caseload - Want the fastest insurance credentialing - Rely on directory traffic to fill your schedule - Don’t mind lower reimbursement in exchange for volume - Can commit to 10+ insurance sessions weekly If those boxes are checked, Try HEADWAY free → and get credentialed in a couple of weeks—no credit card required. --- ## Who should choose Alma? Choose Alma if you: - Want 5–15% higher rates and are okay paying $125/mo - Need Kaiser, TRICARE, or regional plans - Prefer no minimum session quota - Value built-in telehealth and lightweight EHR - Like having a real person to call when something breaks Ready for higher reimbursement and more payer options? Try ALMA free → and try the first month free. --- ## FAQ ### Is Headway or Alma better for therapists who hate administrative work? Headway automates eligibility checks and claims; Alma makes you verify benefits manually. If you want the least admin, Headway wins. ### How long does Alma credentialing take versus Headway? Headway averages 2–4 weeks; Alma runs 4–8 weeks. Start Alma first if you have time before you need to see clients. ### Does Alma really pay more than Headway? On a standard 90834 with BCBS TX, Alma’s negotiated rate is $108 vs Headway’s $98—about 10% higher—though exact rates vary by plan and region. ### Can I use Headway or Alma for private-pay clients? No. Both platforms are in-network only and do not generate superbills or support out-of-network billing. ### Which platform is available in more states, Headway or Alma? Alma operates in all 50 states; Headway is live in 31. --- ## Conclusion If you want the fastest, cheapest way to get on insurance panels and you can keep 10+ weekly sessions full, Headway is the obvious starting point—no monthly fee and a directory that sends clients your way. If you’re willing to pay $125 a month for higher reimbursement, broader payer options, and built-in telehealth plus EHR-lite, Alma gives you more control and higher per-session revenue with no caseload minimum. Decide which trade-off matters more to you, then click below to get started: Start with Headway for free: Try HEADWAY free → Try Alma free for 30 days: Try ALMA free →
